title: 10 best herbal tinctures for allergies

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Butterbur tinctures helps with allergies because its active compounds, petasin and isopetasin, have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. These natural compounds help to reduce inflammation in the body and relieve symptoms associated with allergies such as itching, sneezing, and congestion. By blocking histamine receptors, butterbur also helps to reduce allergic reactions and alleviate symptoms of hay fever, hives, and other allergic conditions. As a result, many people have found relief from their allergy symptoms by using butterbur tinctures in combination with their usual treatment regimens.
remedy_properties: Pyrrolizidine alkaloids (PAs): Help to reduce inflammation and swelling associated with allergic reactions, which can relieve symptoms such as itchiness, redness, and pain.
Essential fatty acids: Support the body's natural anti-inflammatory response and help to regulate the immune system, reducing the severity of allergic reactions.
Flavonoids: Have antioxidant properties that help to neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to the development of allergies and their symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with allergic reactions, such as itching and swelling.

Petasins: These sesquiterpene lactones have been shown to inhibit the release of histamine and other chemical mediators involved in allergic reactions, providing relief from symptoms like congestion and sneezing.

Phenolic acids: These compounds possess antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize free radicals and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating symptoms of allergies and other inflammatory conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part for tinctures due to their high content of petasin and isopetasin, compounds responsible for the plant's anti-inflammatory and antihistamine effects.
Roots: Roots are often used for their similar anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, making them a suitable alternative or complementary part for tinctures.
Flowers: Flowers are sometimes used in tinctures for their ability to interact with the body's histamine receptors, providing relief from allergy symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh petasites hybridus roots and leaves on a sunny day when the plant is in full bloom.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces and place it in a clean glass jar with a lid.
Add 500 milliliters of 35 percent ethanol to the jar and stir the mixture to ensure all parts are saturated.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks to allow the solvent to extract the bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English oak tinctures helps with allergies because of its unique composition, which allows it to target multiple pathways in the body that contribute to allergic responses. The tincture's flavonoids and saponins work together to reduce inflammation, calm histamine release, and stabilize mast cells, all of which can help alleviate symptoms such as itching, congestion, and hives. Additionally, English oak has been shown to increase antioxidant defenses, which helps protect the body from oxidative stress caused by environmental allergens, providing relief for those suffering from allergies.
remedy_properties: Quercetin: A flavonoid that has natural anti-inflammatory and antihistamine effects, helping to reduce itching, swelling, and congestion associated with allergic reactions.
Kaempferol: A flavonoid that acts as a potent antioxidant, neutralizing free radicals and reducing oxidative stress that can exacerbate allergy symptoms such as hay fever and skin rashes.

Vescalagin: A triterpenoid that has been shown to have anti-allergic properties, including inhibiting the release of histamine from mast cells, which helps to alleviate symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and congestion.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids (Quercetin): Quercetin, a flavonoid derived from Quercus robur, is a known anti-inflammatory agent that helps alleviate allergy symptoms by reducing histamine release and preventing mast cell degranulation.

Tannins: Tannins in Quercus robur tinctures have astringent properties that can help alleviate allergy symptoms, particularly those related to nasal congestion and runny nose, by reducing mucus production and promoting its expulsion from the body.

Saponins: Saponins in Quercus robur tinctures have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate allergy symptoms by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which contribute to the development of allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their extracts are used to treat allergies due to their anti-inflammatory properties and ability to reduce histamine levels.
Barks: The bark's extracts are used for its antihistamine and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to alleviate allergy symptoms.
Fruits: The fruit's extracts are used to treat allergies due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of fresh quercus robur leaves and stems, clean them thoroughly with water.
Chop the harvested material into small pieces to increase the surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped quercus robur with 75% vodka in a clean glass jar, ratio 1:3.
Steep the mixture for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily, in a cool, dark place.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, transfer to a dark glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with allergies because of its unique properties. The extract contains a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. When ingested as a tincture, these compounds work to reduce inflammation in the body, alleviating symptoms associated with allergic reactions such as congestion, itchiness, and sneezing. Additionally, the Maidenhair tree's natural antihistamine properties help to calm the body's response to allergens, providing fast and effective relief from allergy symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals, which can exacerbate allergic reactions by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.
Inflammation reduction: The flavonoids present in Maidenhair Tree tincture have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of allergies such as itching, swelling, and redness.

Immune system modulation: Ginkgo biloba has been shown to modulate the immune response, reducing the severity of allergic reactions by regulating the activity of immune cells and suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These polyphenolic compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce histamine release and alleviate allergy symptoms.
Bilobalide: This sesquiterpene lactone has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which help to regulate the immune response and reduce allergic reactions.
Quercetin: A type of flavonoid, quercetin is a potent antioxidant that inhibits histamine release, reduces inflammation, and exhibits anti-allergic properties.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are widely used in tinctures due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which help alleviate allergy symptoms.
Buds: The buds of Ginkgo biloba are used in tinctures because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids that help reduce inflammation and alleviate allergy symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba are used in tinctures due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate allergy symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh ginkgo biloba leaves from mature trees at the end of the growing season.
Clean the leaves thoroughly with distilled water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine the clean leaves with 80 proof vodka at a 1:2 leaf-to-vodka ratio in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks with occasional shaking to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a separate container and discard the solids after 2 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Umckaloabo tinctures helps with allergies because they possess potent anti-inflammatory properties that alleviate symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and congestion. The herb's active compounds, including triterpenoid saponins, have been shown to suppress the production of histamine, a key mediator of allergic reactions. Additionally, umckaloabo has been found to exhibit antioxidant activity, which helps neutralize free radicals that contribute to inflammation. By addressing these underlying mechanisms, umckaloabo tinctures can provide effective relief from allergy symptoms and promote overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Umckaloabo's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as umckalin and isoverbenol, help to reduce swelling and inflammation in the nasal passages and airways, providing relief from congestion and sinus pressure associated with allergies.

Antihistamine-like effects: The tincture's active compounds, including polyphenolic acids and saponins, exhibit antihistamine-like properties that can help to neutralize histamine, a chemical released during allergic reactions, which reduces symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and runny nose.

Mucus thinning and clearance: Umckaloabo's expectorant properties help to break down mucus in the respiratory tract, making it easier to expel, which can reduce congestion and relieve symptoms of allergies such as coughing and phlegm production.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the symptoms associated with allergic reactions, such as swelling and redness.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of allergies by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid is a phenolic compound with strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate the symptoms of allergies by reducing the release of pro-inflammatory mediators and promoting the resolution of inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate allergy symptoms.
Leaves: They are used because they have bioactive compounds that can help reduce inflammation and modulate the immune system.
Stems: They are used because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that can help to reduce histamine release and alleviate allergy symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh pelargonium sidoides roots and leaves in the early morning when the plants are at their highest moisture content.
Dry the harvested roots and leaves in a warm place with good air circulation for 7 to 10 days at 40 degrees celsius.
Grind the dried pelargonium sidoides roots and leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Steep 25 grams of the ground pelargonium sidoides in 500 milliliters of 95% ethanol for 2 weeks in a glass container.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with allergies because it contains potent bioactive compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxerol, which exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the body, which is often a key contributor to allergic reactions. Additionally, dandelion's antihistamine-like effects can help alleviate symptoms of allergic rhinitis, including congestion, itching, and sneezing. By taking an herbal dandelion tincture, individuals may experience relief from allergy-related discomforts and enjoy a healthier respiratory system.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Dandelion tincture contains anti-inflammatory compounds like taraxasterol and taraxeryl acetate, which can help reduce inflammation in the body that can contribute to allergic reactions, such as itching and swelling.

Antihistamine properties: The flavonoids and terpenes present in dandelion tincture have antihistamine-like effects, helping to block histamine release and alleviate symptoms like itching, sneezing, and runny nose associated with allergies.

Immune system support: Dandelion tincture is rich in antioxidants and has immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune response, reduce hypersensitivity reactions, and promote overall well-being, making it a potential natural remedy for allergic conditions.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Taraxacum officinale tinctures have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce histamine release and alleviate allergic symptoms.
Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol, a triterpene, exhibits anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects that can help regulate the immune system and reduce allergic responses.
Chlorogenic acid: Chlorogenic acid, a phenolic acid, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and alleviate allergic symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Taraxacum officinale are used to make tinctures for allergies due to their high content of taraxasterol and other saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale are used to make tinctures for allergies due to their ability to modulate the immune system and reduce inflammation.

Flowers: The flowers of Taraxacum officinale are used to make tinctures for allergies due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest a sufficient quantity of fresh taraxacum officinale leaves and flowers from a clean and pesticide-free area.
Dry the harvested taraxacum officinale in a single layer for at least 24 to 48 hours.
Combine 1 part of dried taraxacum officinale with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks or 2 months.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass jar discarding the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Frankincense tinctures helps with allergies because they contain boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties. These acids work to reduce inflammation in the body, alleviating symptoms such as congestion, itchiness, and sneezing associated with allergic reactions. Additionally, frankincense has natural antihistamine effects, which can help to calm histamine release, further reducing allergy symptoms. By reducing inflammation and histamine release, herbal frankincense tinctures provide a natural solution for alleviating allergy symptoms and promoting overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Frankincense contains boswellic acid, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with allergic reactions, such as congestion and itching.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in frankincense tinctures can help neutralize free radicals that contribute to oxidative stress, which is often exacerbated by allergies and can lead to chronic inflammation and tissue damage.

Immunomodulatory: Frankincense has been found to modulate the immune system's response to allergens, potentially reducing the severity of allergic reactions and promoting a more balanced immune function.
remedy_constituents: Terpenes (Specifically, alpha-pinene and beta-pinene): These terpenes have anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing the inflammation associated with allergic reactions.

Acuminatin: This triterpenoid saponin has potent anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help in alleviating symptoms of allergies by reducing the release of pro-inflammatory mediators.

3-Acetyl-11-keto-beta-boswellic acid (AKBA): This boswellic acid has been shown to inhibit the release of histamine and other chemical mediators involved in allergic responses, thus providing relief from allergy symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Boswellia serrata are primarily used due to their high concentration of boswellic acids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes of Boswellia serrata are utilized because they contain a significant amount of boswellic acids, which help in reducing inflammation and alleviating allergy symptoms.
Barks: The barks of Boswellia serrata are also used in tincture production, as they contain boswellic acids that have anti-inflammatory and anti-allergic properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100 grams of dried boswellia serrata resin and 500 milliliters of 80 percent ethanol.
Combine the dried boswellia serrata resin with 500 milliliters of 80 percent ethanol in a clean glass container.
Steep the mixture for 2 to 4 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discard the solids.
Store the boswellia serrata tincture in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with allergies because it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and itching in the body. The saponins present in licorice root also act as a natural antihistamine, helping to alleviate symptoms such as sneezing, runny nose, and congestion associated with seasonal allergies. Additionally, licorice tincture's mucilages soothe and calm irritated tissues, providing relief from itchy eyes, skin, and respiratory tract irritations.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, helping to reduce symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and congestion associated with allergies.

Licoricidin: This constituent has been shown to inhibit the release of histamine from mast cells, which can help alleviate symptoms like hives, rashes, and itchy eyes and skin associated with allergic reactions.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice tincture have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress that can exacerbate allergy symptoms such as nasal congestion and sinus pressure.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce the allergic response and alleviate symptoms such as itching, congestion, and sneezing.
Glycyrrhizin: A triterpenoid saponin, glycyrrhizin has been found to inhibit the release of histamine, a key mediator of allergic reactions, thereby reducing symptoms associated with allergies.

Flavonoids: Specifically, the flavonoid glycosides in Glycyrrhiza glabra, such as isoliquiritin and liquiritin, have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate allergic reactions and protect against oxidative damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate allergy symptoms.
Leaves: They possess flavonoids and terpenoids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, contributing to their allergy-fighting properties.
Barks: They contain polyphenols and saponins, which help reduce inflammation and have antihistamine-like effects, providing relief from allergy symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh or dried roots in the fall when the plant has finished flowering.
Dry the roots in a warm place with good air circulation for 1 to 2 weeks.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1:5.
Store the mixture in a clean glass jar and steep for 2 to 6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and transfer the tincture to a dark glass bottle for long-term storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow flower tinctures helps with allergies because they contain natural antihistamines that reduce the body's overactive response to allergens. The potent compounds in these flowers, such as flavonoids and terpenes, have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe nasal passages and ease congestion. Additionally, yellow flowers like chamomile and calendula possess antioxidant capabilities that help neutralize free radicals and stabilize the immune system, further alleviating allergy symptoms and promoting a sense of calm. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Yellow flowers like Marigold (Calendula officinalis) and Chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla) contain anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and itching associated with allergic reactions.

Antihistamine properties: Herbs like Yellow Dock (Rumex crispus) and Dandelion (Taraxacum officinale) have natural antihistamine effects, which can help alleviate symptoms such as hives, itching, and sneezing caused by allergies.

Immune-modulating properties: Some yellow flowers, like Echinacea (Echinacea spp.), contain compounds that modulate the immune system, reducing excessive inflammation and allergic responses while promoting a balanced response to allergens.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds may help alleviate allergic reactions by acting as antioxidants, reducing inflammation, and stabilizing mast cells that release histamine, a key player in allergic responses.

Phenolic acids: These compounds, such as gallic acid and ellagic acid, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help mitigate the effects of allergic reactions, such as inflammation and oxidative stress.

Quinones: These compounds, including naphthoquinones, have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities, which may help reduce the severity of allergic responses and alleviate symptoms associated with allergies.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves of Galphimia glauca are used to make tinctures due to their anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties, which help alleviate allergy symptoms.
Stems: Stems of Galphimia glauca are used to make tinctures due to their ability to reduce inflammation and provide relief from respiratory issues associated with allergies.
Barks: Barks of Galphimia glauca are used to make tinctures due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help in soothing allergic reactions and infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the galphimia glauca roots and leaves in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full bloom.
Dry the harvested galphimia glauca plant material in a warm dry place for 2 weeks.
Use a ratio of 1 part galphimia glauca plant material to 2 parts 80 proof vodka to make the tincture.
Steep the galphimia glauca plant material in the vodka mixture for 4 to 6 weeks shaking the mixture daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and bottle it in a dark glass container.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried b monnieri leaves and stems in the morning after the dew has evaporated.
Chop 1 part of the harvested plant material into small pieces using a sharp knife or herb cutter.
Combine the chopped plant material with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tea tinctures helps with allergies because they contain potent, concentrated extracts of herbs that work to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms. Many herbal teas have natural anti-inflammatory properties, such as quercetin found in chamomile, which can help calm the immune system and reduce histamine release. Other herbs like peppermint and ginger have natural decongestant properties that can help clear nasal passages and ease congestion. By incorporating these tea tinctures into your daily routine, you can experience relief from allergy symptoms and breathe easier throughout the year.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbs like turmeric, ginger, and passionflower contain compounds that reduce inflammation in the body, which is often a underlying cause of allergic reactions.

Quercetin: This flavonoid has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help mitigate allergy symptoms by reducing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and mediators.
Theaflavins: These polyphenolic compounds have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help alleviate allergy symptoms by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Camellia sinensis for making tinctures, as they are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help alleviate allergy symptoms.
Buds: Camellia sinensis buds are also used to make tinctures, as they contain a higher concentration of flavonoids and other compounds that help reduce inflammation and alleviate allergy symptoms.
Flowers: Camellia sinensis flowers are used to make tinctures, as they are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids that help soothe and calm the body's response to allergens.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of camellia sinensis leaves from mature tea plants at peak freshness.
Clean the camellia sinensis leaves thoroughly with a soft brush to remove dirt.
Combine 100g of camellia sinensis leaves with 500ml of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 2-6 weeks in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with allergies because it contains bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. These compounds, such as histamine, can bind to receptors in the body, blocking the allergic response and reducing symptoms like itching, sneezing, and congestion. Additionally, stinging nettle has been shown to increase the production of immunoglobulin A (IgA), an antibody that plays a crucial role in the immune system's response to allergens, further aiding in allergy relief.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle tincture helps reduce inflammation, which can alleviate symptoms such as swelling and itching associated with allergic reactions.

Antihistamine properties: The bioactive compounds in stinging nettle, including histamine-binding compounds and flavonoids, help block the action of histamine, a chemical released during allergic responses, reducing symptoms like itching, sneezing, and runny eyes.

Immunomodulatory effects: Stinging nettle tincture may also modulate the immune system's response to allergens, potentially reducing the severity and duration of allergic reactions by regulating the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Clean the harvested material by gently rinsing with distilled water removing any debris or impurities.
Chop the cleaned nettle material into small pieces and mix it with 80 proof vodka at a ratio of 1:2 for exact extraction.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 6-8 weeks in a cool dark place allowing the active compounds to infuse.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle discarding the solids and labeling with date and contents.

remedy_recipe: Harvest euphrasia officinalis flowers during full moon when they are dry and free of contaminants.
Clean and chop the harvested euphrasis officinalis flowers into small pieces using sharp scissors or a herb cutter.
Combine 100 grams of chopped euphrasia officinalis flowers with 600 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for six weeks shaking daily and then strain through cheesecloth into another clean glass container.
Store the final tincture in dark glass bottles and use within two years or until the color changes to yellow.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop tinctures helps with allergies because it contains compounds that have a natural anti-inflammatory effect on the body, which can help to reduce swelling and congestion associated with allergic reactions. Anxiolytic and calming effects: Maypop tincture has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help alleviate anxiety and stress-related allergies, such as hay fever or environmental sensitivities, by promoting relaxation and reducing emotional reactivity.

Combine the harvested dried passionflower with 500ml of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar daily to ensure even extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in the morning.
Chop 100g of the harvested flowers into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 250ml of 30-40% ethanol over the chopped flowers and seal the jar tightly.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar daily at dawn and dusk.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids.

Chop the cleaned fruit into small pieces and combine them with 80 proof vodka in a ratio of 1:4.
Allow the mixture to steep for at least 6 weeks in a cool dark place to allow for extraction.

Store the finished tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years or use within 6 months.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra flowers and leaves in the summer or early fall when they are in full bloom.

remedy_recipe: Harvest althaea officinalis flowers and leaves in late summer or early fall when the plant is in full bloom.
Rinse the harvested plant material gently with clean water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 250 grams of the rinsed althaea officinalis plant material with 1 liter of 70% ethanol vodka in a large glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for six weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing the tincture to steep and extract its medicinal properties.

intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for allergies are concentrated liquid extracts of plants used to alleviate allergy symptoms. They help by reducing inflammation, promoting immune system balance, and providing natural antihistamine properties. The best herbal tinctures for allergies include Urtica dioica, Ginkgo biloba, Echinacea purpurea, Sambucus nigra, Allium sativum, Eucalyptus globulus, Nelumbo nucifera, Quercus robur, Euphorbia peplus, and Tussilago farfara. If left untreated, allergies can significantly impact daily life, causing discomfort, fatigue, and decreased productivity.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to allergies that are treated with herbal tinctures are chronic sinusitis, respiratory issues such as bronchial asthma and coughs, skin irritations like eczema and psoriasis, and gastrointestinal problems like irritable bowel syndrome. Herbal tinctures containing anti-inflammatory compounds like turmeric, ginger, and willow bark can help alleviate symptoms by reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation of the body's natural response to these conditions.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with allergies is a blend of Nettle Leaf, Quercetin, and Butterbur. Nettle Leaf tincture helps to reduce inflammation and itching, while Quercetin, a flavonoid-rich herb, inhibits the release of histamine, a key player in allergic reactions. Butterbur, rich in sesquiterpenes, has anti-inflammatory properties that further alleviate symptoms. This combination supports the body's natural response to allergens, promoting a healthier immune system and reducing the severity of allergy symptoms. Consult with a healthcare professional before using these tinctures.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica tinctures helps with allergies because they contain compounds that reduce inflammation and inhibit the release of histamine, a key player in allergic reactions. The plant's flavonoids and phenolic acids have potent anti-allergic properties, which help to calm the immune system and prevent overactive responses to allergens. By regulating the body's allergic response, Urtica dioica tinctures can provide relief from symptoms such as itching, sneezing, and congestion, making it a natural and effective remedy for managing allergies.
remedy_constituents: Histamine-releasing peptides: These peptides help to reduce the body's allergic response by inhibiting the release of histamine, a key mediator of allergic reactions.

Flavonoids (quercetin, kaempferol, isorhapontigenin): These flavonoids exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with allergic reactions.

Phenolic acids (rosmarinic acid, chlorogenic acid): These phenolic acids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with allergic reactions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of allergen-blocking compounds such as histamine-releasing inhibitors.
Roots: The roots are also used for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate allergy symptoms.
Stems: The stems of Urtica dioica contain flavonoids and other compounds that may help reduce histamine release and alleviate allergic reactions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the urtica dioica plant by gathering fresh leaves and stems from the upper parts of the plant.
Dry the harvested plant material by spreading it out in a single layer on paper bags for 2 weeks.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces using scissors to increase its surface area.
Steep the chopped plant material in a 1:5 ratio with 80% ethanol for 2 weeks in a glass jar.
Filter the liquid tincture using a coffee filter or cheesecloth and store it in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tinctures helps with allergies because they contain flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help reduce the production of histamine, a key player in allergic reactions. By inhibiting the release of histamine, Ginkgo biloba tinctures may alleviate symptoms such as congestion, itching, and sneezing associated with seasonal allergies. Additionally, the flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba may also help to stabilize mast cells, reducing their ability to release histamine and contribute to allergic responses.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the body's allergic response and alleviate symptoms such as itching, congestion, and swelling.
Bilobalide: This terpene derivative can help stabilize mast cell membranes, which are involved in the allergic response, thereby reducing the release of histamine and other inflammatory mediators.
Quercetin: As a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, quercetin can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to allergic reactions and symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Ginkgo biloba are widely used to make tinctures due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: The seeds of Ginkgo biloba are also commonly used to make tinctures due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are thought to provide relief from allergic reactions.
Buds: The buds of Ginkgo biloba are occasionally used to make tinctures due to their high content of flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 ginkgo biloba leaves and stems at the peak of the season or as needed.
Clean and dry the harvested ginkgo biloba material in a low-temperature oven at 100°f for 2 hours.
Combine the dried ginkgo biloba material with 60% vodka in a 1:3 ratio by weight in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tinctures helps with allergies because it contains compounds that support the immune system and reduce inflammation. remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea roots and flowers in the summer months after the plant has reached its full height.
Clean and dry the echinacea purpurea roots and flowers thoroughly to prevent contamination and spoilage.
Combine 1 part of the dried echinacea purpurea roots and flowers with 2 parts of vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow for extraction and infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids after 2 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra tinctures helps with allergies because they contain bioflavonoids and anthocyanins that have powerful anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks with occasional shaking.

remedies.remedy_desc: Allium sativum tinctures helps with allergies because they contain compounds like allicin and ajoene, which possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 pounds of mature allium sativum roots in late summer when the tops have begun to yellow.
Wash the roots thoroughly with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine with 80 proof vodka at a ratio of 1:5.
Store the mixture in a glass jar with a tight-fitting lid in a cool dark place for 4-6 weeks.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus tinctures helps with allergies because its active compound, eucalyptol, has potent anti-inflammatory and decongestant properties. remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves on a dry sunny morning and dry them completely.
Combine the dried leaves with 250 milliliters of 80% ethanol and steep for 2 weeks in a glass jar.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another glass container and press on the solids to extract more.
Discard the solids and add the liquid to a still or a distillation apparatus to concentrate the extract to 25% ethanol.
Transfer the concentrated tincture to dark glass bottles and store them in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar every day or two.
